finding: ATM validation surfaces can grow monotonically and make common flows slower because validators do not yet emit enough per-validator usage, latency, trigger-rate, failure-rate, and defect-interception counters to justify their placement in fast, default, full, or archive tiers.
expectedBehavior: Every validation unit should emit lightweight instrument-mode counters: validator id, mode tier, invocation count, skipped count, duration, failure count, blocking count, downstream defect/incident link when known, and task/surface context. Fast/default/full placement should be evidence-driven. Rarely used or expensive validators should move to full; never-used or no-impact validators should be archived or sampled.
evidenceOrRepro: After ATM-GOV-0185 and ATM-GOV-0195 were marked done, `.atm/runtime/telemetry/analysis-inputs/ATM-GOV-0190-coverage.json` still classifies `validator-queue-execution-cache-fanout` as `not-yet-covered` and lacks validatorId, validatorVersion, durationMs, and fanOutConsumerCount. The final analyzer reports telemetry decisions=0 and no retirement proposal.
followUp: Instrument the validator executor itself, not only aggregate CLI timing. Generate an observed per-validator tier-placement report, apply at least one reversible fast/default/full change under a config digest, and keep archive decisions frequency-aware and owner-reviewed.
